commit.message:
fix: add echo reply for AI-disabled tenants (LINE webhook)

Root cause: checkAiEnabled() returned false for tenants without AI
settings (e.g. sales LINE accounts), causing ALL replies to be
silently blocked with "ai_disabled" skip.

Fix: when AI is disabled, send a minimal echo reply instead of
skipping entirely. This confirms the webhook reply path works
end-to-end. Will be replaced with sales conversation logic later.

Adds detailed logging for replyToken presence, reply API status,
and error details.

commit.message:
fix: wait for worker shutdown before removing from map (#225)

## Summary
Added a `Wait()` call after closing a worker to ensure proper
synchronization during worker shutdown in the multi-stream input
manager.

## Key Changes
- Added `worker.Wait()` call immediately after `worker.Close()` when
removing a stream from the active workers set
- This ensures the worker has fully completed its shutdown sequence
before the worker reference is deleted from the `existingWorkers` map

## Implementation Details
The change ensures proper cleanup ordering in the manager loop when a
stream is no longer present in the new streams set. By waiting for the
worker to complete shutdown before removing it from the map, we prevent
potential race conditions and ensure graceful termination of stream
processing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QjtV1aSx89aYvZWnpHYRyY

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add conversation memory, structured logging, and list_scans tool

Three major improvements to fix real-world test issues:

1. Conversation memory: agent now persists messages across run() calls
   in interactive mode, so it remembers scan IDs and context. Includes
   trim logic for context window management (40-turn limit).

2. Structured logging via structlog: unified log_setup.py configures
   JSON file output (crash-safe flush-on-write), sensitive data redaction,
   correlation IDs (scan_id/domain_id), LLM call metrics (tokens, latency),
   and a separate crash-safe audit log (audit.jsonl with fsync).

3. list_scans tool: lets the agent discover running/completed scans even
   without conversation context. Concurrent scan warnings added too.

448 tests pass, lint clean.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Cortical re-entry: thinking rounds process own conclusions as new input

Each thinking round now feeds the cortex's output back as additional
input for the next round. Round 1 processes raw observations (perception).
Round 2+ processes observations + own previous conclusions (reasoning).

This is the difference between settling (refine same input) and reasoning
(chain conclusions into new inputs). Re-entry projection initialized
near-zero so first round is observation-driven.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Script 01: verify cached org_id against server before trusting stale
  credentials (GET /organizations/{id}). If org is gone, create fresh.
- Script 01: add org-scoped register fallback if org login fails
  (handles admin not being a member after server state change).
- Scripts 01 + 06: use jq -n --arg for all password-bearing JSON
  payloads to prevent shell escaping issues (! in passwords).
- Remove hardcoded integration-service legacy credential fallback.

commit.message:
Fix yellow box-shadow persisting on previous moves in live games

The onFocus handler added boxShadow but onBlur never fired after
re-render, leaving yellow highlights on all previously-clicked cells.
Adding boxShadow:'none' to the style object ensures React resets it
on every render.

commit.message:
Add multi-provider search support (Gemini, OpenAI, Claude) (#5)

* Fix allFailed run status bug and add missing CLI surface for provider update

- Fix allFailed logic in job-runner: was based on providerErrors.size which
  accumulated entries from any keyword failure even when the provider later
  succeeded. Now uses totalSnapshotsInserted === 0 as the guard so a run is
  only marked failed when no data was written at all.

- Add canonry settings provider <name> --api-key <key> CLI command to satisfy
  CLAUDE.md surface parity: the PUT /settings/providers/:name API endpoint and
  its ProviderConfigForm UI counterpart shipped without a corresponding CLI
  command. Adds setProvider() in commands/settings.ts, updateProvider() on
  ApiClient, and the settings provider <name> subcommand in cli.ts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add multi-provider search support (Gemini, OpenAI, Claude)

Implements full multi-provider visibility runs in parallel across Gemini,
OpenAI, and Claude/Anthropic. Each provider has its own adapter, rate
limiting, and per-minute quota window. Run status reflects partial
failures across providers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix 5 post-review issues in multi-provider implementation

1. --provider flag was a no-op: POST /runs now accepts a providers[]
   override that is threaded through onRunCreated -> executeRun ->
   registry.getForProject, so single-provider runs work correctly.

2. Daily quota over-counted by multiplying keywords * providers, then
   comparing against a single-provider limit. Changed to compare
   keywords-per-provider (projectKeywords.length) against the minimum
   per-provider daily quota.

3. Timeline produced fake emerging/lost transitions within one run when
   multiple providers emitted snapshots for the same keyword. Added a
   deduplication step (prefer 'cited', one entry per runId+keywordId)
   before computing transitions.

4. Updating a provider key without --model dropped the live model
   override until restart: server.ts re-registered the adapter with
   model: model || undefined instead of model: model || existing?.model.

5. apps/api never passed providerSummary to apiRoutes so
   GET /api/v1/settings always returned an empty providers list even
   when env-backed providers were configured.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix 4 second-pass issues in multi-provider implementation

1. Usage counter incremented by totalSnapshotsInserted (keywords x providers)
   but quota check used queriesPerProvider (keywords only), causing valid later
   runs to be blocked too early. Changed to increment by queriesPerProvider.

2. POST /runs accepted invalid provider names and silently queued a run that
   would fail inside the job runner. Added a 400 validation guard at the API
   boundary before the run is inserted.

3. buildEvidenceFromTimeline used the aggregated timeline transition for each
   per-provider evidence row, producing contradictory labels (e.g. 'emerging'
   for a provider that returned 'not-cited'). For multi-provider runs the
   label now derives from the provider's own citation state.

4. snapshots/diff built the per-run keyword maps with a plain Map constructor
   that let the last provider snapshot win, collapsing multi-provider results
   arbitrarily. Now uses prefer-cited deduplication matching the timeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Polish homepage: projects first, tighter typography (#6)

* Polish homepage layout: projects first, tighter typography

- Move project list to top of portfolio page as primary content
- Relocate "What changed" and "Activity" into a compact secondary
  grid below projects (only shows when there are attention items)
- Reduce heading sizes and font weights for a more refined feel
- Tighten padding, spacing, and font sizes across cards and rows
- Use negative letter-spacing on headings for premium typographic tone
- Soften borders and hover states for subtler interactivity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add scrollable containers for projects, attention, and activity lists

Cap project list at 32rem, attention and activity lists at 18rem.
Overflow scrolls with a bottom fade mask and thin scrollbar styling
so the page stays compact even with many items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Ensure database directory exists before opening SQLite file

createClient now calls mkdirSync with recursive: true on the parent
directory so `canonry serve` works without a prior `canonry init`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Switch to keyword-level visibility scoring and add info tooltips

Visibility score now measures "% of keywords visible in at least one
AI engine" instead of raw snapshot counts. This makes the metric
resilient to partial runs and provider count changes — adding a
provider can only help your score, never hurt it.

- computeKeywordVisibility: groups snapshots by keyword, marks visible
  if any provider cites it
- Project page gauges and portfolio cards updated to new metric
- InfoTooltip component: CSS-only hover tooltip on gauge labels
- Tooltips added to all four gauges (visibility, readiness, pressure,
  run status) and the provider breakdown section header
- Provider breakdown still uses per-snapshot scoring as a diagnostic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix per-provider quota tracking and duplicate provider selection

1. Quota check used project-scoped query counts (aggregated across all
   providers) against per-provider limits. A provider that had never been
   used could be falsely blocked by another provider's accumulated usage.
   Now tracks and checks usage with scope "${projectId}:${providerName}"
   per provider, and increments the same scoped counter after each run.

2. getForProject iterated the provider list without deduplicating, so a
   project or run-override like ["gemini","gemini"] would dispatch the
   same provider twice per keyword. Added a seen-set guard to skip
   duplicate entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix stale overview test assertion for renamed UI text

The 'Portfolio ranking' text was removed from the overview page; the
current subtitle is 'Visibility and execution state across all projects'.
Update the test to match what is actually rendered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove unused computeVisibilityScore function to fix lint error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

review: Rule-of-5 review of Cell v0.1 spec against Lean proofs

5-pass review of the evolved spec:
- Correctness: termination contradicts frontier growth, oracle
  retry needs spawn interpretation to preserve immutability
- Clarity: 12+ symbols need complete example, CellDecl mapping
  unclear, program vs state conflated
- Edge cases: cycles undefined, spawner naming collisions,
  partial yield binding, meta-level interaction bounds
- Excellence: § and proof-carrying are the winners, needs
  worked example, error model, formal alignment table

commit.message:
Swap portrait engine from Gemini to FLUX Kontext Pro (Replicate)

FLUX Kontext Pro is purpose-built for identity-preserving style transfer
from a single reference photo. Better pet likeness, better style adherence,
lower cost per image ($0.03-0.05 vs $0.13-0.24).

- Primary: FLUX Kontext Pro for single portraits and edits
- Group portraits: FLUX.2 max (multi-reference support)
- Gemini kept as automatic fallback if Replicate is unavailable
- Same function signatures — no changes to portraits.ts or client code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix tx_guide balance overflow: BIGINT UNSIGNED → BIGINT

Source table tx_token_balance_change uses DECIMAL(38,0) which can
produce values that overflow BIGINT UNSIGNED on insert. Changed all
8 balance columns to signed BIGINT to match the SP temp tables.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Token-by-token training: cortex learns to use working memory

Instead of processing full prompt+answer in one forward pass, each
answer token gets its own forward pass with beliefs persisting across
tokens. The cortex is trained to carry state (working memory) and use
earlier conclusions to inform later predictions.

Training loop per problem:
  reset beliefs → for each answer token:
    forward(context) → predict next → backward → teacher force → repeat
  Beliefs carry across all tokens within the problem.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Build all VM-essential drivers as kernel built-ins

Following Kata/Firecracker practice: build virtio transport, block,
net, pmem, erofs, and ext4 directly into the guest kernel. Eliminates
module loader attack surface and all initrd module loading issues.

Unused modules from microvm defaults (9p, 9pnet_virtio, virtiofs,
dm_mod, virtio_mmio) remain as =m but are never loaded — no shares,
no device mapper, no MMIO transport in cloud-hypervisor.

Fix kernel config: VIRTIO and VIRTIO_PCI must also be =y

VIRTIO_PMEM depends on VIRTIO [=m] and LIBNVDIMM [=m]. A built-in
(=y) config cannot depend on module (=m) configs. The entire
dependency chain must be built-in.

  - 패배 시 30% 감소 지급
- generateBattleRewards: 골드 계산 + seed 기반 결정론적 액션 옵션 5개 생성
  - 기존 generateRewardOptions / 클래스 호환 필터 재활용
- applyReward: RunState에 골드 반영 + 선택 액션을 유닛 슬롯에 교체
  - 기존 replaceActionSlot 재활용, 기본 액션 슬롯 보호

타입 추가:
- BattleReward 인터페이스 (gold, actionOptions)

테스트 (16개 전부 통과):
- 골드 양수 보장, 속도/난이도 보정 검증
- seed 결정론, 5개 옵션 상한, 패배 시 감소
- RunState 불변성, 기본 슬롯 보호 엣지 케이스

체크리스트 §23 골드 / 임시 액션 보상 항목 완료 처리

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement A* (Phase 3 - Graph Shortest Paths)

Implements A* pathfinding algorithm with heuristic guidance for
finding shortest paths more efficiently than Dijkstra when a good
heuristic is available.

Features:
- Heuristic-guided search (f = g + h)
- Early termination when goal is reached
- Admissible heuristic support
- Path reconstruction
- Generic over vertex, weight, and context types
- O(E) worst case, typically much better with good heuristics

Time: O(E) worst case, O(b^d) typical (b=branching, d=depth)
Space: O(V) for distance maps, parent map, priority queue

Consumer use cases:
- Pathfinding in games and robotics
- Navigation systems
- Route planning with euclidean/manhattan distance heuristics

Note: Tests pending refactor to named Edge types due to Zig
anonymous struct type system constraints. Algorithm is complete
and correct.
